[Mastering the art of visual storytelling (with examples) | Nulab](https://nulab.com/learn/design-and-ux/visual-storytelling/)

> When you want someone to get the message fast, images work best. That’s because we [process images](https://oit.williams.edu/files/2010/02/using-images-effectively.pdf) a mind-boggling 60,000 times faster than text

Imagine videos?

> **Visual storytelling is the process of sharing a message visually.** It includes images, symbols, colors, movement, and other elements to communicate ideas, elicit emotions, and immerse the audience in a story.

And my job is find out a combination, a new creation from all of that to transmit and preserve the original idea presented by the client through text. *(very fun job if you ask me)*

My form of Visual Storytelling-> **Social media:** Utilizing platforms like Instagram and TikTok for short-form visual narratives

> “Visual storytelling of one kind or another has been around since cavemen were drawing on the walls,” said screenwriter [Frank Darabont](https://www.brainyquote.com/quotes/frank_darabont_217869).

>Visual storytelling is so much more than a way to convey a message, though. It’s a way to grab attention and ultimately reach out to someone.

Such an insane Copy I could use to sell my service

![{22CDA9CB-3AA1-425E-BC96-7686B45D5CE3}.png](data:image/png;base64,)

This blog with all its details should be quoted.
